reddit_title: Got hit by a semi-truck in Plano, TX — do I actually need a lawyer or can I handle the insurance claim myself?
reddit_body:
Truck accident cases in Texas are genuinely more complicated than standard car accident claims, and it's worth understanding why before deciding to go it alone. Commercial trucks fall under federal FMCSA regulations, and violations of those rules — hours of service, maintenance logs, weight limits — can be central to proving liability. That evidence, including black box and electronic logging device data, can be requested or even destroyed quickly, so timing matters a lot.

Another thing people don't realize is that there are often multiple liable parties in a truck accident: the driver, the trucking company, a third-party cargo loader, or even the vehicle manufacturer. Each of those parties will have their own insurance adjusters and attorneys working to minimize your payout from day one. Having someone in your corner who knows how to deal with that is genuinely valuable.

If you're trying to figure out your next steps, it's worth at least consulting with a truck accident attorney before accepting any settlement offer. Sites like wreckmatch.com can connect you with attorneys in your area who specifically handle these types of cases — no pressure, just a way to understand your options quickly.
